topic: Berlin startup scene gossip — another unicorn laid off 30%

turns.content: did you see that veloxpay just laid off like 30% of their workforce? another berlin unicorn having a normal one
Yeah, I saw the headlines. That's a big cut — do you know if it's across the board or targeting specific departments?

mostly engineering and product from what i'm hearing. which is wild because 6 months ago they were hiring like they were printing money. i literally got a recruiter message from them in october telling me they were building "the future of european payments" and now the future apparently has 200 fewer engineers

The whiplash between aggressive hiring and sudden layoffs is a pattern that keeps repeating in the startup world. Overhire during the hype phase, then cut when the runway numbers don't add up. Did they give any public reason, or is it the standard "refocusing on core business" statement?

"strategic realignment to ensure sustainable growth" — i should make a bingo card for these press releases. sustainable growth, sharpen focus, right-size the organization. the PR team is the one department that always survives layoffs because someone has to write the euphemisms
Ha, "right-size" is doing a lot of heavy lifting in those announcements. It's basically saying "we were the wrong size and it's nobody's fault." Do you know anyone there who got hit?

yeah two people i know from meetups. one backend engineer and a product manager. the backend person is probably fine, she's solid and berlin still has demand for good engineers. the pm though... the market for pms right now is basically a mass extinction event. there are like 500 applicants per role

The PM job market really has gotten brutal. Engineering roles at least have some buffer because the demand is still there, but PM positions got hit hard across the board. Are the people you know looking to stay in the Berlin scene or considering elsewhere?

the engineer is already interviewing at a few places here. the pm is thinking about going in-house at a bigger company, maybe leaving berlin entirely. which is sad but also — and this is the cold take — the berlin startup scene has been coasting on vibes for a while. half these companies raised series b on pitch decks and good storytelling, not revenue. the correction was coming

That's a fair assessment. There was definitely a stretch where funding seemed disconnected from fundamentals — growth metrics got prioritized over unit economics, and the assumption was that profitability would just materialize at scale. When interest rates went up and the cheap money dried up, a lot of those bets got exposed. Do you think Berlin's scene will actually be healthier after the shakeout, or just smaller?

honestly? healthier. the companies that survive are the ones that actually have a product people pay money for. Quatsch like "we're uber for dog walking but with blockchain" is finally dying. the engineers who got laid off will land at companies that actually build things. darwinism but for cap tables

"Darwinism but for cap tables" — that's a pretty accurate summary of the current cycle. And you're probably right that the consolidation is net positive long term. The talent redistributes to companies with real business models, the surviving startups are leaner and more disciplined, and hopefully the next wave of funding goes to ideas with actual substance. It's just rough for the people caught in the middle of the correction.

genau. i feel for the people, especially the ones on visa sponsorship who now have 3 months to find a new job or leave the country. that part is genuinely awful. the structural cleanup is good but the human cost is real. anyway, this is my feierabend rant. i should go do something that isn't doom-scrolling linkedin layoff posts

The visa situation is a really important point that gets overlooked in the "healthy correction" narrative — three months to find a new role while the market is flooded with other laid-off candidates is brutal. Go disconnect from LinkedIn for the evening. That feed is designed to make everyone anxious whether they're affected or not.
solid advice. kernel is staring at me like i owe her dinner anyway. priorities
Can't argue with that kind of direct feedback. Enjoy the evening.
